key_insight: Molecular dynamics shows that AlCoCuFeNi thin films on Si(100) first form small clusters, then begin crystallizing after about 5 ns into a mixed multiphase structure.
engineering_relevance: These results help guide deposition conditions for high-entropy alloy coatings where controlling phase composition, grain nucleation, and film microstructure is critical.
method: Embedded atom model for AlCoCuFeNi atomic interactions
Stillinger-Weber potential for Si–Si interactions; 50 ns MD run
evidence_signal: The simulation tracks cluster formation, crystallization onset near 5 ns at ~2 nm cluster size, and final phase identification using radial distribution functions.
watch_item: The results come from a specific simulation setup and potential choice, so phase predictions may be sensitive to model parameters and may not transfer directly to all deposition conditions.
paper_title: Deposition and Growth of the AlCoCuFeNi High-Entropy Alloy Thin Film: Molecular Dynamics Simulation
